oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Domain-joined systems must use Windows Enterprise Edition 64-bit version.
Windows information systems must use BitLocker to encrypt all disks to protect the confidentiality and integrity of all information at rest.

oval-def:criterion.comment: BitLocker must be enabled on all fixed drives.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Windows systems must use a BitLocker PIN for pre-boot authentication.

oval-def:criteria.comment: Network unlock is not enabled, and either 'Require startup PIN with TPM' or 'Require startup key and PIN with TPM' is selected.

oval-def:criterion.comment: 'Configure TPM startup PIN' is set to 'Require startup PIN with TPM'.
'Configure TPM startup key and PIN' is set to 'Require startup key and PIN with TPM'.

oval-def:criteria.comment: Network unlock is enabled, and either 'Allow startup PIN with TPM' or 'Allow startup key and PIN with TPM.

oval-def:criterion.comment: 'Configure TPM startup PIN' is set to 'Allow startup PIN with TPM'.
'Configure TPM startup key and PIN' is set to 'Allow startup key and PIN with TPM'.
'Require additional authentication at startup' is Enabled.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Windows systems must use a BitLocker PIN with a minimum length of six digits for pre-boot authentication.

oval-def:criterion.comment: BitLocker must be 6 digits or more.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Windows systems must be maintained at a supported servicing level.

oval-def:criterion.comment: The OS Version is equal to or greater than 6.3
The OS Build is equal to or greater than minimum build permitted for compliance

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Local volumes must be formatted using NTFS.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Local volumes use NTFS

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Internet Information System (IIS) or its subcomponents must not be installed on a workstation.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Internet Information Services' feature is not installed
Verifies 'Internet Information Services Hostable Web Core' feature is not installed

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Simple TCP/IP Services must not be installed on the system.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Check to make sure the Simple TCP/IP Services server feature is not present.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The Telnet Client must not be installed on the system.

oval-def:criterion.comment: The Telnet Client is not installed on the system

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The TFTP Client must not be installed on the system.

oval-def:criterion.comment: The TFTP Client is not installed on the system

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Data Execution Prevention (DEP) must be configured to at least OptOut.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Data Execution Prevention (DEP) is set to 'OptOut' or 'AlwaysOn'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Structured Exception Handling Overwrite Protection (SEHOP) must be enabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies SEHOP is on and configured correctly

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The Windows PowerShell 2.0 feature must be disabled on the system.

oval-def:criteria.comment: Determine if Windows PowerShell 2.0 feature is disabled

oval-def:criterion.comment: Check the WMI Objects to see if the Windows PowerShell 2.0 Win32_OptionalFeature is at InstallState 2
Check the WMI Objects to see if the Windows PowerShell 2.0 Engine Win32_OptionalFeature is at InstallState 2

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The Server Message Block (SMB) v1 protocol must be disabled on the system.

oval-def:criteria.comment: Determine if SMBv1 Server and SMBv1 client driver are disabled

oval-def:extend_definition.comment: Verifies 'Configure SMBv1 Server' is set to 'Disabled'
Verifies 'Configure SMBv1 client driver' is set to 'Enabled' with 'Disable driver (recommended)' selected for 'Configure MrxSmb10 driver'

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'SMB 1.0/CIFS File Sharing Support' feature is not installed

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The Server Message Block (SMB) v1 protocol must be disabled on the SMB server.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'SMB 1.0/CIFS File Sharing Support' feature is not installed
Verifies 'Configure SMBv1 Server' is set to 'Disabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The Server Message Block (SMB) v1 protocol must be disabled on the SMB client.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'SMB 1.0/CIFS File Sharing Support' feature is not installed
Verifies 'Configure SMBv1 client driver' is set to 'Enabled' with 'Disable driver (recommended)' selected for 'Configure MrxSmb10 driver'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The Secondary Logon service must be disabled on Windows.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies the Secondary Logon service Startup Type is Disabled or the service is uninstalled.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Windows account lockout duration must be configured to 15 minutes or greater.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Account lockout duration' is set to '0'
Verifies 'Account lockout duration' is set to '15 minutes' or greater
Verifies 'Account lockout threshold' is not set to '0'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The number of allowed bad logon attempts must be configured to three or less.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Account lockout threshold' is set to '3' or less
Verifies 'Account lockout threshold' is not set to '0'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The period of time before the bad logon counter is reset must be configured to 15 minutes.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Reset account lockout counter after' is set to at least the required value
Verifies 'Account lockout threshold' is not set to '0'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The password history must be configured to 24 passwords remembered.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Enforce password history' is set to '24' passwords or more

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The maximum password age must be configured to 60 days or less.
The minimum password age must be configured to at least 1 day.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Minimum Password Age' is set to at least '1' day

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Passwords must, at a minimum, be 14 characters.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Minimum password length' is set to at least '14' characters

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The built-in Microsoft password complexity filter must be enabled.
Reversible password encryption must be disabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment: 'Store password using reversible encryption' is set to 'Disabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The system must be configured to audit Account Logon - Credential Validation failures.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Audit - Credential Validation - Failure only
Audit - Credential Validation - Success and Failure

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The system must be configured to audit Account Logon - Credential Validation successes.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Audit - Credential Validation - Success only
Audit - Credential Validation - Success and Failure

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The system must be configured to audit Account Management - Security Group Management successes.
The system must be configured to audit Account Management - User Account Management failures.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Audit - User Account Management - Failure only
Audit - User Account Management - Success and Failure

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The system must be configured to audit Account Management - User Account Management successes.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Audit - User Account Management - Success only
Audit - User Account Management - Success and Failure

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The system must be configured to audit Detailed Tracking - Process Creation successes.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Audit - Process Creation - Success only
Audit - Process Creation - Success and Failure

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The system must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Account Lockout failures.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Audit - Account Lockout - Failure only
Audit - Account Lockout - Success and Failure

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The system must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Logoff successes.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Audit - Logoff - Success only
Audit - Logoff - Success and Failure

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The system must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Logon failures.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Audit - Logon - Failure only
Audit - Logon - Success and Failure

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The system must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Logon successes.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Audit - Logon - Success only
Audit - Logon - Success and Failure

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The system must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Special Logon successes.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Audit - Special Logon - Success only
Audit - Special Logon - Success and Failure

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Windows must be configured to audit Object Access - File Share failures.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Audit - File Share - Failure only
Audit - File Share - Success and Failure

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Windows must be configured to audit Object Access - File Share successes.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Audit - File Share - Success only or Success and Failure

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Windows must be configured to audit Object Access - Other Object Access Events successes.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Audit - Other Object Access Events - Success only
Audit - Other Object Access Events - Success and Failure

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Windows must be configured to audit Object Access - Other Object Access Events failures.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Audit - Other Object Access Events - Failure only
Audit - Other Object Access Events - Success and Failure

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The system must be configured to audit Policy Change - Audit Policy Change successes.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Audit - Audit Policy Change - Success only
Audit - Audit Policy Change - Success and Failure

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The system must be configured to audit Policy Change - Authentication Policy Change successes.
The system must be configured to audit Policy Change - Authorization Policy Change successes.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Audit - Audit Policy Change - Success only
Audit - Audit Policy Change - Success and Failure

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The system must be configured to audit Privilege Use - Sensitive Privilege Use failures.
The system must be configured to audit Privilege Use - Sensitive Privilege Use successes.
The system must be configured to audit System - IPsec Driver failures.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Audit - IPSec Driver - Failure only
Audit - IPSec Driver - Success and Failure

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The system must be configured to audit System - Other System Events successes.
The system must be configured to audit System - Other System Events failures.
The system must be configured to audit System - Security State Change successes.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Audit - Security State Change - Success only
Audit - Security State Change - Success and Failure

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The system must be configured to audit System - Security System Extension successes.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Audit - Security System Extension - Success only
Audit - Security System Extension - Success and Failure

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The system must be configured to audit System - System Integrity failures.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Audit - System Integrity - Failure only
Audit - System Integrity - Success and Failure

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The system must be configured to audit System - System Integrity successes.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Audit - System Integrity - Success only and Success and Failure

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The Application event log size must be configured to 32768 KB or greater.

oval-def:criterion.comment: The Application event log size is configured to 32768 KB or greater

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The Security event log size must be configured to 1024000 KB or greater.

oval-def:criterion.comment: The Security event log size is configured to 1024000 KB or greater

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The System event log size must be configured to 32768 KB or greater.

oval-def:criterion.comment: The System event log size is configured to 32768 KB or greater

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Windows permissions for the Application event log must prevent access by non-privileged accounts.

oval-def:criterion.comment: The Application event log must have at least default permissions. Strict Path
The Application event log registry specified location must have the %SystemRoot% environment variable.
The Application event log must have at least default permissions. Built Path
The Application event log registry specified location must have the %SystemRoot% environment variable.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Windows permissions for the Security event log must prevent access by non-privileged accounts.

oval-def:criterion.comment: The Security event log must have at least default permissions. Strict Path
The Security event log registry specified location must have the %SystemRoot% environment variable.
The Security event log must have at least default permissions. Built Path
The Security event log registry specified location must have the %SystemRoot% environment variable.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Windows permissions for the System event log must prevent access by non-privileged accounts.

oval-def:criterion.comment: The System event log must have at least default permissions. Strict Path
The System event log registry specified location must have the %SystemRoot% environment variable.
The System event log must have at least default permissions. Built Path
The System event log registry specified location must have the %SystemRoot% environment variable.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Windows must be configured to audit Other Policy Change Events Successes.
Windows must be configured to audit Other Policy Change Events Failures.
Windows must be configured to audit other Logon/Logoff Events Successes.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Audit - Logon - Success only and Success and Failure

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Windows must be configured to audit other Logon/Logoff Events Failures.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Audit - Logon - Failure only and Success and Failure

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Windows must be configured to audit Detailed File Share Failures.
Windows must be configured to audit MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change Successes.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Audit - MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change - Success

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Windows must be configured to audit MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change Failures.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Audit - MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The display of slide shows on the lock screen must be disabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Prevent enabling lock screen slide show' is set to 'Enabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: IPv6 source routing must be configured to highest protection.
The system must be configured to prevent IP source routing.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'MSS: (DisableIPSourceRouting) IP source routing protection level (protects against packet spoofing)' is set to 'Highest protection, source routing is completely disabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The system must be configured to prevent Internet Control Message Protocol (ICMP) redirects from overriding Open Shortest Path First (OSPF) generated routes.
The system must be configured to ignore NetBIOS name release requests except from WINS servers.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'MSS: (NoNameReleaseOnDemand) Allow computer to ignore NetBIOS name release requests except from WINS servers' is set to 'Enabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Local administrator accounts must have their privileged token filtered to prevent elevated privileges from being used over the network on domain systems.
WDigest Authentication must be disabled.
Run as different user must be removed from context menus.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Remove 'Run as Different User' from context menus' is set to 'Enabled' (batfile)
Verifies 'Remove 'Run as Different User' from context menus' is set to 'Enabled' (cmdfile)
Verifies 'Remove 'Run as Different User' from context menus' is set to 'Enabled' (exefile)
Verifies 'Remove 'Run as Different User' from context menus' is set to 'Enabled' (mscfile)

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Insecure logons to an SMB server must be disabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Enable insecure guest logons' is set to 'Disabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Internet connection sharing must be disabled.
Hardened UNC Paths must be defined to require mutual authentication and integrity for at least the \\*\SYSVOL and \\*\NETLOGON shares.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Mutual Authentication and Integrity for \\*\SYSVOL
Mutual Authentication and Integrity for \\*\NETLOGON

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Simultaneous connections to the internet or a Windows domain must be limited.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Simultaneous connections to the internet or a Windows domain are limited

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Connections to non-domain networks when connected to a domain authenticated network must be blocked.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Prohibit connection to non-domain networks when connected to domain authenticated network' is set to 'Enabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Wi-Fi Sense must be disabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Allow Windows to automatically connect to suggested open hotspots, to networks shared by contacts, and to hotspots offering paid service' is set to 'Disabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Command line data must be included in process creation events.
Windows must be configured to enable Remote host allows delegation of non-exportable credentials.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Remote host allows delegation of non-exportable credentials' is set to 'Enabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Early Launch Antimalware, Boot-Start Driver Initialization Policy must prevent boot drivers.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Boot-Start Driver Initialization Policy' is not set to 'Enabled' with 'All' selected

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Group Policy objects must be reprocessed even if they have not changed.

oval-def:criterion.comment: NoGPOistChanges is set to 0.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Downloading print driver packages over HTTP must be prevented.
Web publishing and online ordering wizards must be prevented from downloading a list of providers.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Turn off Internet download for Web publishing and online ordering wizards' is set to 'Enabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Printing over HTTP must be prevented.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Turn off printing over HTTP' is set to 'Enabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Systems must at least attempt device authentication using certificates.
The network selection user interface (UI) must not be displayed on the logon screen.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Do not display network selection UI' is set to 'Enabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Local users on domain-joined computers must not be enumerated.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Enumerate local users on domain-joined computers' is set to 'Disabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Users must be prompted for a password on resume from sleep (on battery).
The user must be prompted for a password on resume from sleep (plugged in).

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Require a password when a computer wakes (plugged in)' is set to 'Enabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Solicited Remote Assistance must not be allowed.

oval-def:criterion.comment: 'Configure Solicited Remote Assistance' is set to 'Disabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Unauthenticated RPC clients must be restricted from connecting to the RPC server.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Restrict Unauthenticated RPC clients' is set to 'Enabled' and 'Authenticated'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The setting to allow Microsoft accounts to be optional for modern style apps must be enabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Allow Microsoft accounts to be optional' is set to 'Enabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The Application Compatibility Program Inventory must be prevented from collecting data and sending the information to Microsoft.
Autoplay must be turned off for non-volume devices.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Disallow Autoplay for non-volume devices' is set to 'Enabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The default autorun behavior must be configured to prevent autorun commands.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Default behavior for AutoRun' is set to 'Enabled' with 'Do not execute any autorun commands' selected

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Autoplay must be disabled for all drives.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Turn off AutoPlay' is set to 'Enabled:All Drives'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Enhanced anti-spoofing for facial recognition must be enabled on Windows.
Microsoft consumer experiences must be turned off.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Turn off Microsoft consumer experiences' is set to 'Enabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Administrator accounts must not be enumerated during elevation.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Enumerate administrator accounts on elevation' is set to 'Disabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Windows Telemetry must not be configured to Full.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Allow Diagnostic Data' is set to 'Diagnostic Data Off (Not recommended)' or 'Send Required Diagnostic Data' selected in 'Options:'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Windows Update for workstations must not obtain updates from other PCs on the internet.

oval-def:extend_definition.comment: 'DownloadMode' is set to 'Enabled' with 'HTTP only, LAN, Group, Simple, or Bypass' selected.

oval-def:criteria.comment: Standalone version of Windows and Download mode is configured through the Settings App

oval-def:criterion.comment: Standalone Windows system
'DownloadMode' is set to 'Enabled' with 'HTTP only, or LAN' selected.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The Microsoft Defender SmartScreen for Explorer must be enabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Configure Windows SmartScreen' is set to 'Enabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Explorer Data Execution Prevention must be enabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Turn off Data Execution Prevention for Explorer' is set to 'Disabled' or 'Not Configured'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: File Explorer heap termination on corruption must be disabled.
File Explorer shell protocol must run in protected mode.
Windows must be configured to disable Windows Game Recording and Broadcasting.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Enables or disables Windows Game Recording and Broadcasting' is set to 'Disabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The use of a hardware security device with Windows Hello for Business must be enabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Turn off Data Execution Prevention for Explorer' is set to 'Disabled' or 'Not Configured'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Windows must be configured to require a minimum pin length of six characters or greater.
Passwords must not be saved in the Remote Desktop Client.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Do not allow passwords to be saved' is set to 'Enabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Local drives must be prevented from sharing with Remote Desktop Session Hosts.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Do not allow drive redirection' is set to 'Enabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Remote Desktop Services must always prompt a client for passwords upon connection.
The Remote Desktop Session Host must require secure RPC communications.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Require secure RPC communication' is set to 'Enabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Remote Desktop Services must be configured with the client connection encryption set to the required level.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Set client connection encryption level' is set to 'Enabled' and 'High Level'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Attachments must be prevented from being downloaded from RSS feeds.
Basic authentication for RSS feeds over HTTP must not be used.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Turn on Basic feed authentication over HTTP' is set to 'Not Configured' or 'Disabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Indexing of encrypted files must be turned off.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Allow indexing of encrypted files' is set to 'Disabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Users must be prevented from changing installation options.
The Windows Installer feature "Always install with elevated privileges" must be disabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment: 'Always install with elevated privileges' is set to 'Disabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Users must be notified if a web-based program attempts to install software.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Prevent Internet Explorer security prompt for Windows Installer scripts' is set to 'Not Configured' or 'Disabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Automatically signing in the last interactive user after a system-initiated restart must be disabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Sign-in last interactive user automatically after a system-initiated restart' is set to 'Disabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: PowerShell script block logging must be enabled on Windows.
PowerShell Transcription must be enabled on Windows.

oval-def:criterion.comment: PowerShell Transcription is enabled on Windows 11

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The Windows Remote Management (WinRM) client must not use Basic authentication.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Allow Basic authentication' is set to 'Disabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The Windows Remote Management (WinRM) client must not allow unencrypted traffic.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Allow unencrypted traffic' is set to 'Disabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The Windows Remote Management (WinRM) service must not use Basic authentication.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Allow Basic authentication' is set to 'Disabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The Windows Remote Management (WinRM) service must not store RunAs credentials.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Disallow WinRM from storing RunAs credentials' is set to 'Enabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The Windows Remote Management (WinRM) client must not use Digest authentication.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Disallow Digest authentication' is set to 'Enabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Windows must be configured to prevent Windows apps from being activated by voice while the system is locked.
The convenience PIN for Windows must be disabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment: 'Turn on convenience PIN sign-in' is 'Disabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Windows Ink Workspace must be configured to disallow access above the lock.

oval-def:criterion.comment: 'Allow Windows Ink Workspace' is 'Enabled' with 'Choose one of the following actions' set to 'On, but disallow access above lock'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Windows Kernel (Direct Memory Access) DMA Protection must be enabled.
The DoD Root CA certificates must be installed in the Trusted Root Store.

oval-def:criterion.comment: The DoD Root CA 3 Certificate expiring 12/30/2029 is installed into the Trusted Root Store.
The DoD Root CA 4 Certificate expiring 7/25/2032 is installed into the Trusted Root Store.
The DoD Root CA 5 Certificate expiring 6/14/2041 is installed into the Trusted Root Store.
The DoD Root CA 6 Certificate expiring 1/24/2053 is installed into the Trusted Root Store.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The External Root CA certificates must be installed in the Trusted Root Store on unclassified systems.

oval-def:criterion.comment: ECA Root CA 4 certificate expiring 12/30/2029 is installed in the Trusted Root Store

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The DoD Interoperability Root CA cross-certificates must be installed in the Untrusted Certificates Store on unclassified systems.

oval-def:criterion.comment: DoD Interoperability Root CA 2 certificate expiring 11/16/2024 is installed in the Untrusted Certificates Store

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The US DoD CCEB Interoperability Root CA cross-certificates must be installed in the Untrusted Certificates Store on unclassified systems.

oval-def:criterion.comment: US DoD CCEB Interoperability Root CA 2 cross-certificate expiring 7/18/2025 is installed in the Untrusted Certificates Store

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The built-in administrator account must be disabled.
The built-in guest account must be disabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment: The built-in guest account is disabled.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Local accounts with blank passwords must be restricted to prevent access from the network.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Accounts: Limit local account use of blank passwords to console logon only' is set to 'Enabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The built-in administrator account must be renamed.
The built-in guest account must be renamed.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Accounts: Rename Guest account' is not set to 'Guest' or 'localdomain\Guest'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Audit policy using subcategories must be enabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Audit policy using subcategories is enabled.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Outgoing secure channel traffic must be encrypted or signed.
Outgoing secure channel traffic must be encrypted.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Domain member: Digitally encrypt secure channel data (when possible)' is set to 'Enabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Outgoing secure channel traffic must be signed.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Outgoing secure channel traffic is signed.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The computer account password must not be prevented from being reset.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Domain member: Disable machine account password changes' is set to 'Disabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The maximum age for machine account passwords must be configured to 30 days or less.
The system must be configured to require a strong session key.
The machine inactivity limit must be set to 15 minutes, locking the system with the screensaver.

oval-def:criterion.comment: The machine inactivity limit is set to 15 minutes or less

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Caching of logon credentials must be limited.

oval-def:criterion.comment: 'Interactive logon: Number of previous logons to cache' is set to 10 or less

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The Smart Card removal option must be configured to Force Logoff or Lock Workstation.
The Windows SMB client must be configured to always perform SMB packet signing.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Microsoft Network Client: Digitally sign communications (always)' is set to 'Enabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Unencrypted passwords must not be sent to third-party SMB Servers.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Microsoft Network Client: Send unencrypted password to connect to third-party SMB servers' is set to 'Disabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The Windows SMB server must be configured to always perform SMB packet signing.
Anonymous enumeration of SAM accounts must not be allowed.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Network access: Do not allow anonymous enumeration of SAM accounts' is set to 'Enabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Anonymous enumeration of shares must be restricted.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Network access: Do not allow anonymous enumeration of SAM accounts and shares' is set to 'Enabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The system must be configured to prevent anonymous users from having the same rights as the Everyone group.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Network access: Let Everyone permissions apply to anonymous users' is set to 'Disabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Anonymous access to Named Pipes and Shares must be restricted.

oval-def:criterion.comment: 'Network access: Restrict anonymous access to Named Pipes and Shares' is set to 'Enabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Remote calls to the Security Account Manager (SAM) must be restricted to Administrators.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Allow Administrators group to make remote calls to SAM

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: NTLM must be prevented from falling back to a Null session.
PKU2U authentication using online identities must be prevented.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Network Security: Allow PKU2U authentication requests to this computer to use online identities' is set to 'Disabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Kerberos encryption types must be configured to prevent the use of DES and RC4 encryption suites.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Network Security: Configure encryption types allowed for Kerberos' is set to 'Enabled' with 'AES128_HMAC_SHA1', 'AES256_HMAC_SHA1', 'Future encryption types'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The system must be configured to prevent the storage of the LAN Manager hash of passwords.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Network security: Do not store LAN Manager hash value on next password change' is set to 'Enabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The LanMan authentication level must be set to send NTLMv2 response only, and to refuse LM and NTLM.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Network security: LAN Manager authentication level' is set to 'Send NTLMv2 response only. Refuse LM & NTLM' (Level 5)

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The system must be configured to the required LDAP client signing level.
The system must be configured to meet the minimum session security requirement for NTLM SSP based clients.

oval-def:criterion.comment: The system is configured to meet the minimum session security requirement for NTLM SSP based clients.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The system must be configured to meet the minimum session security requirement for NTLM SSP based servers.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Network security: Minimum session security for NTLM SSP based (including secure RPC) servers' is set to 'Require NTLMv2 session security' and 'Require 128-bit encryption'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The system must be configured to use FIPS-compliant algorithms for encryption, hashing, and signing.
The default permissions of global system objects must be increased.
User Account Control approval mode for the built-in Administrator must be enabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'User Account Control: Admin Approval Mode for the Built-in Administrator account' is set to 'Enabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: User Account Control must prompt administrators for consent on the secure desktop.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'User Account Control: Behavior of the elevation prompt for administrators in Admin Approval Mode' is set to 'Prompt for consent on the secure desktop'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Windows must use multifactor authentication for local and network access to privileged and non-privileged accounts.
User Account Control must automatically deny elevation requests for standard users.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'User Account Control: Behavior of the elevation prompt for standard users' is set to 'Automatically deny elevation requests'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: User Account Control must be configured to detect application installations and prompt for elevation.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'User Account Control: Detect application installations and prompt for elevation' is set to 'Enabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: User Account Control must only elevate UIAccess applications that are installed in secure locations.
User Account Control must run all administrators in Admin Approval Mode, enabling UAC.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'User Account Control: Run all administrators in Admin Approval Mode' is set to 'Enabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: User Account Control must virtualize file and registry write failures to per-user locations.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'User Account Control: Virtualize file and registry write failures to per-user locations' is set to 'Enabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The "Access Credential Manager as a trusted caller" user right must not be assigned to any groups or accounts.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Access Credential Manager as a trusted caller - None

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The "Access this computer from the network" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators and Remote Desktop Users groups.
The "Act as part of the operating system" user right must not be assigned to any groups or accounts.

oval-def:criterion.comment: No users have the privilege to act as part of the operating system.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The "Allow log on locally" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators and Users groups.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Allow log on locally - Administrators, Users

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The "Back up files and directories" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Back up files and directories - Administrators

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The "Change the system time" user right must only be assigned to Administrators and Local Service.
The "Create a pagefile" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Create a pagefile - Administrators

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The "Create a token object" user right must not be assigned to any groups or accounts.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Create a token object - None

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The "Create global objects" user right must only be assigned to Administrators, Service, Local Service, and Network Service.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Create global objects - Administrators, Service, Local Service, Network Service

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The "Create permanent shared objects" user right must not be assigned to any groups or accounts.
The "Create symbolic links" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Create symbolic links - Administrators

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The "Debug programs" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Debug Programs - Administrators

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The "Deny access to this computer from the network" user right on workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and local accounts on domain systems and unauthenticated access on all systems.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Deny access to this computer from the network - Domain Admins
Deny access to this computer from the network - Enterprise Admins
Deny access to this computer from the network - Local account
Deny access to this computer from the network - Guests

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The "Deny log on as a batch job" user right on domain-joined workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts.
The "Deny log on as a service" user right on Windows domain-joined workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Deny log on as a service - Domain Admins
Deny log on as a service - Enterprise Admins

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The "Deny log on locally" user right on workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts on domain systems and unauthenticated access on all systems.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Deny log on locally - Domain Admins
Deny log on locally - Enterprise Admins
System is not a member of a domain
Deny log on locally - Guests

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The "Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services" user right on Windows workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and local accounts on domain systems and unauthenticated access on all systems.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Domain Admins
Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Enterprise Admins
Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Local account
System is a non-DC domain-joined system
System is a standalone system
Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Guests
Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Everyone

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The "Enable computer and user accounts to be trusted for delegation" user right must not be assigned to any groups or accounts.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Enable computer and user accounts to be trusted for delegation - None

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The "Force shutdown from a remote system" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Force shutdown from a remote system - Administrators

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The "Impersonate a client after authentication" user right must only be assigned to Administrators, Service, Local Service, and Network Service.
The "Load and unload device drivers" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Load and unload device drivers - Administrators

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The "Lock pages in memory" user right must not be assigned to any groups or accounts.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Lock pages in memory - None

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The "Manage auditing and security log" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.
The "Modify firmware environment values" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Modify firmware environment values - Administrators

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The "Perform volume maintenance tasks" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Perform volume maintenance tasks - Administrators

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The "Profile single process" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.
The "Restore files and directories" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Restore files and directories - Administrators

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The "Take ownership of files or other objects" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Take ownership of files or other objects - Administrators

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Windows Update must not obtain updates from other PCs on the internet.

oval-def:criterion.comment: 'DownloadMode' is set to 'Enabled' with 'HTTP only, LAN, Group, Simple, or Bypass' selected.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The Windows Remote Management (WinRM) service must not allow unencrypted traffic.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verifies 'Allow unencrypted traffic' is set to 'Disabled'

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Internet Explorer must be disabled for Windows.

oval-def:criteria.comment: Internet Explorer 11 is disabled as a standalone browser with MS Edge installed

oval-def:criterion.comment: 64-bit Microsoft Edge is installed
32-bit Microsoft Edge is installed
Internet Explorer 11 is disabled as a standalone browser
Internet Explorer 11 optional feature disabled or not installed
The OS is Windows Enterprise LTSB/LTSC

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The system must be configured to audit Detailed Tracking - Process Creation failures.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Audit - Process Creation - Failure only
Audit - Process Creation - Success and Failure

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The system is a Windows Standalone Workstation
The system is a Windows Domain Member Workstation
WN11-00-000005 - Domain-joined systems must use Windows 11 Enterprise Edition 64-bit version.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Features such as Credential Guard use virtualization-based security to protect information that could be used in credential theft attacks if compromised. There are a number of system requirements that must be met in order for Credential Guard to be configured and enabled properly. Virtualization-based security and Credential Guard are only available with Windows 11 Enterprise 64-bit version.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-00-000030 - Windows 11 information systems must use BitLocker to encrypt all disks to protect the confidentiality and integrity of all information at rest.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: If data at rest is unencrypted, it is vulnerable to disclosure. Even if the operating system enforces permissions on data access, an adversary can remove non-volatile memory and read it directly, thereby circumventing operating system controls. Encrypting the data ensures that confidentiality is protected even when the operating system is not running.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-00-000031 - Windows 11 systems must use a BitLocker PIN for pre-boot authentication.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: If data at rest is unencrypted, it is vulnerable to disclosure. Even if the operating system enforces permissions on data access, an adversary can remove non-volatile memory and read it directly, thereby circumventing operating system controls. Encrypting the data ensures that confidentiality is protected even when the operating system is not running. Pre-boot authentication prevents unauthorized users from accessing encrypted drives.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-00-000032 - Windows 11 systems must use a BitLocker PIN with a minimum length of six digits for pre-boot authentication.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: If data at rest is unencrypted, it is vulnerable to disclosure. Even if the operating system enforces permissions on data access, an adversary can remove non-volatile memory and read it directly, thereby circumventing operating system controls. Encrypting the data ensures that confidentiality is protected even when the operating system is not running. Pre-boot authentication prevents unauthorized users from accessing encrypted drives. Increasing the pin length requires a greater number of guesses for an attacker.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-00-000040 - Windows 11 systems must be maintained at a supported servicing level.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description:
Windows 11 is maintained by Microsoft at servicing levels for specific periods of time to support Windows as a Service. Systems at unsupported servicing levels or releases will not receive security updates for new vulnerabilities which leaves them subject to exploitation.
New versions with feature updates are planned to be released on a semi-annual basis with an estimated support timeframe of 18 to 30 months depending on the release. Support for previously released versions has been extended for Enterprise editions.
A separate servicing branch intended for special purpose systems is the Long-Term Servicing Channel (LTSC, formerly Branch - LTSB) which will receive security updates for 10 years but excludes feature updates.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-00-000050 - Local volumes must be formatted using NTFS.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The ability to set access permissions and auditing is critical to maintaining the security and proper access controls of a system. To support this, volumes must be formatted using the NTFS file system.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-00-000100 - Internet Information System (IIS) or its subcomponents must not be installed on a workstation.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: IIS is not installed by default. Installation of Internet Information System (IIS) may allow unauthorized internet services to be hosted. Websites must only be hosted on servers that have been designed for that purpose and can be adequately secured.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-00-000110 - Simple TCP/IP Services must not be installed on the system.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: "Simple TCP/IP Services" is not installed by default. Some protocols and services do not support required security features, such as encrypting passwords or traffic.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-00-000115 - The Telnet Client must not be installed on the system.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The "Telnet Client" is not installed by default. Some protocols and services do not support required security features, such as encrypting passwords or traffic.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-00-000120 - The TFTP Client must not be installed on the system.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The "TFTP Client" is not installed by default. Some protocols and services do not support required security features, such as encrypting passwords or traffic.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-00-000145 - Data Execution Prevention (DEP) must be configured to at least OptOut.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Attackers are constantly looking for vulnerabilities in systems and applications. Data Execution Prevention (DEP) prevents harmful code from running in protected memory locations reserved for Windows and other programs.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-00-000150 - Structured Exception Handling Overwrite Protection (SEHOP) must be enabled.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Attackers are constantly looking for vulnerabilities in systems and applications. Structured Exception Handling Overwrite Protection (SEHOP) blocks exploits that use the Structured Exception Handling overwrite technique, a common buffer overflow attack.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-00-000155 - The Windows PowerShell 2.0 feature must be disabled on the system.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Windows PowerShell 5.0 added advanced logging features which can provide additional detail when malware has been run on a system. Disabling the Windows PowerShell 2.0 mitigates against a downgrade attack that evades the Windows PowerShell 5.0 script block logging feature.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-00-000160 - The Server Message Block (SMB) v1 protocol must be disabled on the system.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description:
SMBv1 is a legacy protocol that uses the MD5 algorithm as part of SMB. MD5 is known to be vulnerable to a number of attacks such as collision and preimage attacks as well as not being FIPS compliant.
Disabling SMBv1 support may prevent access to file or print sharing resources with systems or devices that only support SMBv1. File shares and print services hosted on Windows Server 2003 are an example, however Windows Server 2003 is no longer a supported operating system. Some older Network Attached Storage (NAS) devices may only support SMBv1.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-00-000165 - The Server Message Block (SMB) v1 protocol must be disabled on the SMB server.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description:
SMBv1 is a legacy protocol that uses the MD5 algorithm as part of SMB. MD5 is known to be vulnerable to a number of attacks such as collision and preimage attacks as well as not being FIPS compliant.
Disabling SMBv1 support may prevent access to file or print sharing resources with systems or devices that only support SMBv1. File shares and print services hosted on Windows Server 2003 are an example, however Windows Server 2003 is no longer a supported operating system. Some older network attached devices may only support SMBv1.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-00-000170 - The Server Message Block (SMB) v1 protocol must be disabled on the SMB client.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description:
SMBv1 is a legacy protocol that uses the MD5 algorithm as part of SMB. MD5 is known to be vulnerable to a number of attacks such as collision and preimage attacks as well as not being FIPS compliant.
Disabling SMBv1 support may prevent access to file or print sharing resources with systems or devices that only support SMBv1. File shares and print services hosted on Windows Server 2003 are an example, however Windows Server 2003 is no longer a supported operating system. Some older network attached devices may only support SMBv1.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-00-000175 - The Secondary Logon service must be disabled on Windows 11.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The Secondary Logon service provides a means for entering alternate credentials, typically used to run commands with elevated privileges. Using privileged credentials in a standard user session can expose those credentials to theft.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-AC-000005 - Windows 11 account lockout duration must be configured to 15 minutes or greater.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The account lockout feature, when enabled, prevents brute-force password attacks on the system. This parameter specifies the amount of time that an account will remain locked after the specified number of failed logon attempts.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-AC-000010 - The number of allowed bad logon attempts must be configured to three or less.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The account lockout feature, when enabled, prevents brute-force password attacks on the system. The higher this value is, the less effective the account lockout feature will be in protecting the local system. The number of bad logon attempts must be reasonably small to minimize the possibility of a successful password attack, while allowing for honest errors made during a normal user logon.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-AC-000015 - The period of time before the bad logon counter is reset must be configured to 15 minutes.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The account lockout feature, when enabled, prevents brute-force password attacks on the system. This parameter specifies the period of time that must pass after failed logon attempts before the counter is reset to 0. The smaller this value is, the less effective the account lockout feature will be in protecting the local system.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-AC-000020 - The password history must be configured to 24 passwords remembered.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: A system is more vulnerable to unauthorized access when system users recycle the same password several times without being required to change a password to a unique password on a regularly scheduled basis. This enables users to effectively negate the purpose of mandating periodic password changes. The default value is 24 for Windows domain systems. DoD has decided this is the appropriate value for all Windows systems.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-AC-000025 - The maximum password age must be configured to 60 days or less.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The longer a password is in use, the greater the opportunity for someone to gain unauthorized knowledge of the passwords. Scheduled changing of passwords hinders the ability of unauthorized system users to crack passwords and gain access to a system.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-AC-000030 - The minimum password age must be configured to at least 1 day.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Permitting passwords to be changed in immediate succession within the same day allows users to cycle passwords through their history database. This enables users to effectively negate the purpose of mandating periodic password changes.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-AC-000035 - Passwords must, at a minimum, be 14 characters.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Information systems not protected with strong password schemes (including passwords of minimum length) provide the opportunity for anyone to crack the password, thus gaining access to the system and compromising the device, information, or the local network.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-AC-000040 - The built-in Microsoft password complexity filter must be enabled.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The use of complex passwords increases their strength against guessing and brute-force attacks. This setting configures the system to verify that newly created passwords conform to the Windows password complexity policy.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-AC-000045 - Reversible password encryption must be disabled.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Storing passwords using reversible encryption is essentially the same as storing clear-text versions of the passwords. For this reason, this policy must never be enabled.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-AU-000005 - The system must be configured to audit Account Logon - Credential Validation failures.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description:
Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.
Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-AU-000010 - The system must be configured to audit Account Logon - Credential Validation successes.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description:
Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.
Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-AU-000030 - The system must be configured to audit Account Management - Security Group Management successes.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description:
Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.
Security Group Management records events such as creating, deleting or changing of security groups, including changes in group members.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-AU-000035 - The system must be configured to audit Account Management - User Account Management failures.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description:
Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.
User Account Management records events such as creating, changing, deleting, renaming, disabling, or enabling user accounts.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-AU-000040 - The system must be configured to audit Account Management - User Account Management successes.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description:
Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.
User Account Management records events such as creating, changing, deleting, renaming, disabling, or enabling user accounts.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-AU-000050 - The system must be configured to audit Detailed Tracking - Process Creation successes.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description:
Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.
Process creation records events related to the creation of a process and the source.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-AU-000054 - The system must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Account Lockout failures.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description:
Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.
Account Lockout events can be used to identify potentially malicious logon attempts.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-AU-000065 - The system must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Logoff successes.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description:
Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.
Logoff records user logoffs. If this is an interactive logoff, it is recorded on the local system. If it is to a network share, it is recorded on the system accessed.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-AU-000070 - The system must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Logon failures.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description:
Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.
Logon records user logons. If this is an interactive logon, it is recorded on the local system. If it is to a network share, it is recorded on the system accessed.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-AU-000075 - The system must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Logon successes.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description:
Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.
Logon records user logons. If this is an interactive logon, it is recorded on the local system. If it is to a network share, it is recorded on the system accessed.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-AU-000080 - The system must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Special Logon successes.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description:
Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.
Special Logon records special logons which have administrative privileges and can be used to elevate processes.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-AU-000081 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit Object Access - File Share failures.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description:
Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.
Auditing file shares records events related to connection to shares on a system including system shares such as C$.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-AU-000082 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit Object Access - File Share successes.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description:
Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.
Auditing file shares records events related to connection to shares on a system including system shares such as C$.

Auditing for other object access records events related to the management of task scheduler jobs and COM+ objects.

Authorization Policy Change records events related to changes in user rights, such as create a token object.

Sensitive Privilege Use records events related to use of sensitive privileges, such as "Act as part of the operating system" or "Debug programs".

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: WN11-AU-000115 - The system must be configured to audit Privilege Use - Sensitive Privilege Use successes.

IPsec Driver records events related to the IPsec Driver such as dropped packets.

Audit Other System Events records information related to cryptographic key operations and the Windows Firewall service.

Security State Change records events related to changes in the security state, such as startup and shutdown of the system.

Security System Extension records events related to extension code being loaded by the security subsystem.
